paper_metadata.title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment
paper_metadata.abstract: Given the broad capabilities of large language models, it should be possible to work towards a general-purpose, text-based assistant that is aligned with human values, meaning that it is helpful, honest, and harmless. As an initial foray in this direction we study simple baseline techniques and evaluations, such as prompting. We find that the benefits from modest interventions increase with model size, generalize to a variety of alignment evaluations, and do not compromise the performance of large models. Next we investigate scaling trends for several training objectives relevant to alignment, comparing imitation learning, binary discrimination, and ranked preference modeling. We find that ranked preference modeling performs much better than imitation learning, and often scales more favorably with model size. In contrast, binary discrimination typically performs and scales very similarly to imitation learning. Finally we study a `preference model pre-training' stage of training, with the goal of improving sample efficiency when finetuning on human preferences.

extraction_data.innovation: Preference Model Pre-training (PMP) as a method to improve sample efficiency and asymptotic performance of preference models for alignment tasks.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (including Stack Exchange, Reddit, Wikipedia, and Python code)
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving sample efficiency and performance of preference models for alignment tasks.
extraction_data.application: Alignment-focused tasks, preference modeling, reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF).
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

extraction_data.innovation: PMP (Preference Model Pre-training) is a method that uses human-human comparisons to train a language model to align with human preferences. It involves finetuning on human-human comparisons (e.g., Stack Exchange, Reddit) and then evaluating on diverse tasks like summarization and ethics.
extraction_data.application: Alignment evaluation, preference modeling, ethical AI
extraction_data.research_problem: Technical AI alignment, training language models to be helpful, honest, and harmless.
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

extraction_data.innovation: Human-model comparisons involve replacing 'bad' samples in human-human datasets with model-generated samples to create a dataset for finetuning. This approach is hypothesized to improve transfer to downstream tasks with model-generated data.
extraction_data.application: Alignment evaluation, preference modeling, ethical AI
extraction_data.research_problem: Alignment evaluation using human-model comparisons to assess model behavior on diverse tasks.
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

extraction_data.innovation: PMP involves comparing human-human and human-model pairs to evaluate alignment. Human-model comparisons involve replacing 'bad' samples with model-generated data to assess transfer to tasks with model-generated data.
extraction_data.application: Alignment evaluation, preference modeling, ethical AI
extraction_data.research_problem: Alignment evaluation using human-human and human-model comparisons to assess model behavior on diverse tasks.
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment
